name: pressure_fed_lox_kerosene_system_d44ac686
description: Design a pressure-fed rocket engine feed system using LOX (liquid oxygen) and kerosene as propellants, with nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant gas. The system must include: GN2 tank for pressurization, LOX tank, kerosene tank, a rocket engine block, pressurization lines from GN2 to each propellant tank, and feed lines from each tank to the engine. FIXED: component architecture (gn2_tank, lox_tank, kerosene_tank, engine, gn2_to_lox_pressurization, gn2_to_kerosene_pressurization, lox_feed_line, kerosene_feed_line). TUNABLE: tank pressures, volumes, initial masses, line diameters, and flow rates. The system must establish stable flow through both feed lines to the engine.

notes: Use the pressure_fed_lox_kerosene seed as the starting template
Ensure GN2 pressurization maintains sufficient pressure in both propellant tanks
Both LOX and kerosene must flow to the engine during operation
Preserve the Engine node and the two feed Series connections into it.
Tune feed CdA values before changing topology.
If physical warnings mention a component, fix that component first.

checks.description: Simulation must complete without crashing or invalid configuration
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
LOX feed line must have non-zero mass flow during operation
Kerosene feed line must have non-zero mass flow during operation
GN2 to LOX pressurization line must have non-zero mass flow
GN2 to kerosene pressurization line must have non-zero mass flow
Engine chamber pressure must be positive (above atmospheric)
LOX tank pressure must remain positive throughout simulation
Kerosene tank pressure must remain positive throughout simulation
GN2 tank pressure must remain positive throughout simulation
LOX feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
Kerosene feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
